twitter_thread: 73% of households can't make ends meet. That's 90 million households needing a side hustle.
High-demand skills like AI, stats, and writing pay $50-100/hr. Not $10/hr dog walking.
Upwork, Fiverr, and Freelancer have 10 million clients. Offer services like content creation or data analysis.
I've seen people make $1,000/mo on Fiverr. Don't sell yourself short with low-ball offers.
Coursera's AI course has a 95% completion rate. Invest 2-3 months, earn a skill that pays.
Sell ebooks on Amazon Kindle or create courses on Udemy. Digital products can earn $5,000/mo.
What's the highest-paying side hustle you've found? #affordabilitycrisis #sidehustle
pack.linkedin: 73% of households need a side hustle, totaling 90 million. Focus on in-demand skills like AI and statistics, which can pay $50-100/hr. Utilize platforms like Upwork and Fiverr, and develop skills through Coursera or Udemy. What strategies have you used to increase your income by $1,000/mo or more?
tiktok_hooks: 90 million households can't pay the bills without a side hustle
Want a $50/hr side hustle? Learn AI or stats
Tired of living paycheck to paycheck? Create a digital product
pack.reddit_headline: Are Side Hustles the New Baseline for Middle-Class Survival?
